name: smiles-drawer

en.when_to_use: Use it instead of assembling canvas/SVG by hand when you need to render a SMILES string as a readable 2D molecular structure, because native drawing fails at ring layout, bond angles, aromaticity, element labels, and overlap resolution.
en.why_valuable: AI can generate SMILES text by default but cannot produce trustworthy chemical-structure layout at all; hand-built molecule diagrams get bond angles, rings, and labels wrong. smiles-drawer closes the parsing, graph-layout, and canvas/SVG drawing loop in the browser.

schema.smiles: string -- one molecule SMILES; this entry does not use the reaction SMILES branch

spec.note: canvas defaults to 480x320px, with width and height written to both canvas attributes and Drawer options; vh/percentage height is prohibited. For responsive behavior, the consuming AI must draw again with new integer pixel dimensions.

when_to_use: 需要把 SMILES 字符串渲成可读的 2D 分子结构式时用它,而非手拼 canvas/SVG——原生绘制在环布局、键角、芳香性、元素标签和重叠消解上失败。
why_valuable: AI 默认能生成 SMILES 文本却完全做不到可信的化学结构布局,手拼分子图会画错键角、环和标签；smiles-drawer 把解析、图布局与 canvas/SVG 绘制闭环在浏览器内。

data_in._why_no_example: SMILES 是 AI 原生记法,schema 足矣,不附 minimal example。

schema.smiles: string —— 一个 molecule SMILES；本 entry 不走 reaction SMILES 分支
schema.width?: positive integer CSS pixels —— 默认 480
schema.height?: positive integer CSS pixels —— 默认 320

spec.note: canvas 默认 480×320px,宽高同时写 canvas attribute 与 Drawer options；禁止 vh/百分比高。需要响应式时由消费 AI 按新的整数 pixel 尺寸重新 draw。

boot.codex: 同 artifact(sandbox iframe):default ESM import → new SmilesDrawer.Drawer(options) → SmilesDrawer.parse → drawer.draw(tree, canvasElement, theme, false)。

carriers.widget: supported:parse 纯 JS,draw 可直接吃 shadow 内 HTMLCanvasElement；无 CSS/head/图片/font CDN 注入

isolation.evidence: Verified vs jsDelivr smiles-drawer@2.4.1: `Jn as default` and `window.SmilesDrawer=Jn` — the default export is the SmilesDrawer namespace. dist/types/app.d.ts confirms `export default class SmilesDrawerNS` with static Drawer, static parse(smiles, successCallback, errorCallback?), Version, apply, clean, parseReaction — so `import SmilesDrawer from` then `new SmilesDrawer.Drawer(options)` and `SmilesDrawer.parse(...)` are correct. Drawer.d.ts: `constructor(options)` and `draw(data, target: string|HTMLCanvasElement, themeName?, infoOnly?, highlight_atoms?)` matches boot `drawer.draw(tree, canvas, theme ?? 'light', false)`. Schema smiles/theme/width/height consumed correctly. Isolation: +esm shows 0 document.head/body/createElement('style')/document.addEventListener/window.addEventListener — parse is pure JS, draw renders into the shadow-mounted HTMLCanvasElement. Only side effect is assigning window.SmilesDrawer/window.SmiDrawer globals (harmless namespace write, not a DOM/style/event footprint).
